name: WebGazer.js
tagline: Browser-based eye tracking library using common webcams
short_impression: WebGazer.js is an open-source JavaScript library that enables real-time eye tracking directly in web browsers using standard webcams. Developed at Brown University's HCI lab, it has become the most widely adopted webcam-based eye tracking solution for web applications, with peer-reviewed validation studies demonstrating accuracy suitable for many research paradigms.
pros: No installation required - runs entirely in browser
Compatible with major frameworks (React, Vue, vanilla JS)
cons: Requires HTTPS for webcam access in production

papers.title: WebGazer: Scalable Webcam Eye Tracking Using User Interactions
papers.authors: Papoutsaki, A., Sangkloy, P., Laskey, J., Daskalova, N., Huang, J., & Hays, J.

claimed_accuracy: Approximately 1-2 degrees of visual angle under optimal conditions (peer-reviewed)
privacy_notes: All processing occurs client-side in the browser. No data is sent to external servers by default.
